annotations.label: Left R-module

anchor.snippet: A left R -module M consists of an abelian group

annotations.note: `Module R M` over a `Semiring R` and `AddCommMonoid M` is exactly a left module.

annotations.label: Right R-module

anchor.snippet: A right R -module M R is defined similarly in terms of an operation

annotations.note: Mathlib models right modules as left modules over the opposite ring, i.e. `Module Rᵐᵒᵖ M`.

annotations.label: Bimodule

anchor.snippet: bimodule is an abelian group together with both a left scalar multiplication

annotations.note: Bimodules have no dedicated class but are expressed as `[Module R M] [Module Sᵐᵒᵖ M] [SMulCommClass R Sᵐᵒᵖ M]`, as documented in this file.

annotations.label: Modules over commutative rings

anchor.snippet: then left R -modules are the same as right R -modules

annotations.note: No theorem states the identification of left and right modules over a commutative ring (Mathlib simply uses left modules throughout).

annotations.label: K-modules are vector spaces

anchor.snippet: If K is a field , then K -modules are called K - vector spaces

annotations.note: A vector space is `Module K V` with `K` a field/division ring, the special case of `Module` over a field.

annotations.label: K[x]-modules

anchor.snippet: then a K [ x ]-module M is a K -module with an additional action of x

annotations.note: `Module.AEval` is the `R[X]`-module structure on a module given by an algebra element acting as `X`.

annotations.label: Z-modules are abelian groups

anchor.snippet: The concept of a Z -module agrees with the notion of an abelian group.

annotations.note: `AddCommGroup.uniqueIntModule` shows every abelian group carries a unique ℤ-module structure.

annotations.label: Decimal fractions module

anchor.snippet: The decimal fractions (including negative ones) form a module over the integers.

annotations.note: The specific ℤ-module of decimal fractions (ℤ[1/10]) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free module R^n

anchor.snippet: then the cartesian product R n is both a left and right R -module

annotations.note: `Rⁿ = Fin n → R` has `Pi.module` structure and is a `Module.Free R` instance.

annotations.label: Matrix ring modules

anchor.snippet: If M n ( R ) is the ring of n × n matrices over a ring R

annotations.note: The matrix ring is `Matrix.instRing` and acts on `Fin n → R` via `Matrix.mulVec`, but `Rⁿ` as a named module over `Mₙ(R)` is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Module of functions M^S

anchor.snippet: is the collection of all functions

annotations.note: `Pi.module` gives the module structure on (dependent) function types, generalizing `M^S = S → M`.

annotations.label: Modules over smooth functions

anchor.snippet: If X is a smooth manifold , then the smooth functions from X to the real numbers form a ring

annotations.note: Smooth functions form a (comm)ring via instances on `ContMDiffMap`, but modules over them are not separately developed.

annotations.label: Ideals as modules

anchor.snippet: If R is any ring and I is any left ideal in R , then I is a left R -module

annotations.note: An ideal is `Ideal R := Submodule R R`, i.e. a submodule of `R` viewed as a module over itself.

annotations.label: Opposite ring modules

anchor.snippet: we can define the opposite ring R op

annotations.note: `MulOpposite` (`Rᵐᵒᵖ`) exists and right modules are realized as `Module Rᵐᵒᵖ M`.

annotations.label: Lie algebra modules

anchor.snippet: Modules over a Lie algebra are (associative algebra) modules over its universal enveloping algebra .

annotations.note: Both `LieModule` and `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra` are formalized, but the equivalence between Lie modules and modules over the enveloping algebra is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Restriction of scalars

anchor.snippet: then every S -module M is an R -module

annotations.note: `Module.compHom` (and `RestrictScalars`) turns an S-module into an R-module along a ring homomorphism.

annotations.label: Submodule

anchor.snippet: Then N is a submodule (or more explicitly an R -submodule) if for any n in N and any r in R

annotations.note: `Submodule R M` is the bundled subset closed under addition and scalar multiplication.

annotations.label: Submodule spanned by a set

anchor.snippet: then the submodule spanned by X is defined to be

annotations.note: `Submodule.span R s` is the smallest submodule containing the set `s`.

annotations.label: Lattice of submodules and modular law

anchor.snippet: forms a lattice that satisfies the modular law

annotations.note: Submodules form a complete lattice with an `IsModularLattice (Submodule R M)` instance.

annotations.label: Module homomorphism

anchor.snippet: then a map f : M → N is a homomorphism of R -modules if

annotations.note: `LinearMap` is the bundled additive, scalar-preserving map between modules.

annotations.label: Module isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A bijective module homomorphism f : M → N is called a module isomorphism

annotations.note: `LinearEquiv` (`M ≃ₗ[R] N`) is a bijective linear map, i.e. a module isomorphism.

annotations.label: Kernel and image

anchor.snippet: The kernel of a module homomorphism f : M → N is the submodule of M

annotations.note: `LinearMap.ker` (and `LinearMap.range` in the sibling file) define kernel and image as submodules.

annotations.label: Isomorphism theorems for modules

anchor.snippet: The isomorphism theorems familiar from groups and vector spaces are also valid for R -modules.

annotations.note: The first/second/third isomorphism theorems are `quotKerEquivRange`, `quotientInfEquivSupQuotient`, and `quotientQuotientEquivQuotient`.

annotations.label: R-Mod is an abelian category

anchor.snippet: the set of all left R -modules together with their module homomorphisms forms an abelian category

annotations.note: `ModuleCat.abelian` provides the `Abelian (ModuleCat R)` instance.

annotations.label: Finitely generated module

anchor.snippet: An R -module M is finitely generated if there exist finitely many elements

annotations.note: `Module.Finite` states the top submodule is finitely generated (`Submodule.FG`).

annotations.label: Cyclic module

anchor.snippet: A module is called a cyclic module if it is generated by one element.

annotations.note: Cyclicity is expressible as `(⊤ : Submodule R M).IsPrincipal`, but there is no dedicated cyclic-module class.

annotations.label: Free module

anchor.snippet: A free R -module is a module that has a basis

annotations.note: `Module.Free R M` is the class asserting `M` admits a basis.

annotations.label: Projective module

anchor.snippet: Projective modules are direct summands of free modules

annotations.note: `Module.Projective` is the class of projective modules.

annotations.label: Injective module

anchor.snippet: Injective modules are defined dually to projective modules.

annotations.note: `Module.Injective` is the class of injective modules.

annotations.label: Flat module

anchor.snippet: A module is called flat if taking the tensor product

annotations.note: `Module.Flat` is the class defined via exactness of tensoring.

annotations.label: Torsionless module

anchor.snippet: A module is called torsionless if it embeds into its algebraic dual .

annotations.note: No torsionless-module predicate (embedding into the double dual) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simple module

anchor.snippet: A simple module S is a module that is not {0}

annotations.note: `IsSimpleModule` is a nontrivial module whose only submodules are `⊥` and `⊤`.

annotations.label: Semisimple module

anchor.snippet: A semisimple module is a direct sum (finite or not) of simple modules.

annotations.note: `IsSemisimpleModule` is the class of (complemented) semisimple modules.

annotations.label: Indecomposable module

anchor.snippet: An indecomposable module is a non-zero module that cannot be written as a direct sum

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsMulIndecomposable` for monoids but no indecomposable-module predicate.

annotations.label: Faithful module

anchor.snippet: A faithful module M is one where the action of each r ≠ 0 in R on M is nontrivial

annotations.note: `FaithfulSMul R M` is exactly the condition that distinct scalars act differently.

annotations.label: Torsion-free module

anchor.snippet: A torsion-free module is a module over a ring such that 0 is the only element annihilated

annotations.note: `Module.IsTorsionFree` is the torsion-free predicate (cf. also `NoZeroSMulDivisors`).

annotations.label: Noetherian module

anchor.snippet: A Noetherian module is a module that satisfies the ascending chain condition on submodules

annotations.note: `IsNoetherian R M` is the ascending chain / well-foundedness condition on submodules.

annotations.label: Artinian module

anchor.snippet: An Artinian module is a module that satisfies the descending chain condition on submodules

annotations.note: `IsArtinian R M` is the descending chain condition on submodules.

annotations.label: Graded module

anchor.snippet: A graded module is a module over a graded ring

annotations.note: Internal graded modules are set up via `SetLike.GradedSMul` plus `DirectSum.Decomposition`.

annotations.label: Uniform module

anchor.snippet: A uniform module is a module in which all pairs of nonzero submodules have nonzero intersection.

annotations.note: No uniform-module predicate (nonzero submodules intersecting nontrivially) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group representation as module

anchor.snippet: A representation of a group G over a field k is a module over the group ring k [ G ].

annotations.note: `Representation k G V` corresponds to a `Module k[G]` structure via `Representation.asModule`.

annotations.label: Action of a ring element

anchor.snippet: then the action of an element r in R is defined to be the map

annotations.note: `Module.toAddMonoidEnd R M r` is the endomorphism `x ↦ r • x`.

annotations.label: Representation of abelian group over a ring

anchor.snippet: is called a representation of the abelian group M over the ring R

annotations.note: `Module.toAddMonoidEnd : R →+* AddMonoid.End M` is precisely the representation/ring-hom to endomorphisms.

annotations.label: Faithful representation

anchor.snippet: A representation is called faithful if the map

annotations.note: Faithfulness (injectivity of the action map) is captured by `FaithfulSMul`, but there is no dedicated faithful-representation definition.

annotations.label: Module over a preadditive category

anchor.snippet: a left R -module is just a covariant additive functor from R to the category of abelian groups

annotations.note: Additive functors exist, but a module as a covariant additive functor on a (one-object) preadditive category is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Sheaf of modules

anchor.snippet: consider the sheaves of O X -modules

annotations.note: `SheafOfModules` is the structure of sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings.

annotations.label: Semimodule

anchor.snippet: One can also consider modules over a semiring , called a semimodule .

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Module` is already defined over a `Semiring` and `AddCommMonoid`, so semimodules are exactly `Module`.

annotations.label: Near-ring module

anchor.snippet: one can consider near-ring modules, a nonabelian generalization of modules.

annotations.note: Near-rings and near-ring modules are not present in Mathlib.
